guidingPrinciple: Data is the memory of the community.

philosophies.entity: Stable ID, ownership, lifecycle, status, visibility, timestamps
philosophies.time: Preserve change — current state and journey both matter
philosophies.event: Important actions produce immutable event records
philosophies.configuration: Platform behavior rules — governed separately from operational data
philosophies.operational: Community activity facts — preserved as institutional memory
philosophies.publicFacts: External truth with source attribution and periodic review
philosophies.ownership: Domain services own their records — Registry, Community, Mission, Experience, Story, Knowledge, Growth, Identity
philosophies.stewardship: Review, approve, attribute, dedupe, protect privacy, document revisions
philosophies.versioning: Charters, canvas, playbooks, profiles, policies, templates, knowledge, stories
philosophies.status: Explicit lifecycle — planned through archived; history preserved
philosophies.visibility: Separate from existence — evaluated through permission system
philosophies.privacy: Consent, minimization, export, deletion/archival, sensitive audit
philosophies.audit: Actor, action, target, timestamp, before/after, source, reason
philosophies.search: Projection of canonical data — never bypass authorization
philosophies.ai: Derived only — sources, confidence, explanation, human approval where needed
philosophies.analytics: Interpretations with documented source, method, refresh, limits, scope
philosophies.archive: Institutional memory retained; deletion for privacy/safety/legal/dedup only

expansionRule: Additive evolution — more institution types, communities, relationships, events, knowledge, states, AI capabilities
